seoTitle: Marks Landing vs Marleston — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Marks Landing and Marleston. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Marks Landing vs Marleston: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Marks Landing and Marleston.
heading: Marks Landing vs Marleston — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Marks Landing, South Australia and Marleston, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Marks Landing has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Marleston (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Marks Landing or Marleston safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Marks Landing has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Marleston scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Marks Landing scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Marleston scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Marks Landing or Marleston?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Marks Landing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
